text.title: vr-design-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the vr-design-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every VR scene MUST have a visible ground plane AND a stable horizon. Horizonless or constantly-rotating scenes are forbidden as the default.

rationale.source: Meta Quest comfort docs; Oculus VRC.PC.UX

rule.rationale: Vestibular system anchors to ground + horizon; their absence is the leading cause of nausea.

rule.statement: Default locomotion MUST be teleportation for novice or general audiences. Smooth locomotion MAY be available as a user-selectable option, never the default for new users.
rule.rationale: Smooth locomotion triggers nausea in ~40% of novice users; teleport rates ~5%.

rule.statement: Primary UI MUST be world-anchored or body-anchored, NOT head-locked. Head-locked HUD is reserved for safety telemetry only.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Meta Quest UI guidelines

rule.rationale: Head-locked UI breaks immersion and triggers vestibular conflict.

rule.statement: App MUST ship a seated-mode toggle AND a one-handed control path. Both MUST be reachable from the main menu.
rule.rationale: Seated and one-handed paths are mandatory for accessibility and store approval.

rule.statement: Subtitles MUST be available and discoverable via the comfort menu. Audio descriptions for visually-encoded meaning MUST be authored.

rationale.source: XR Access Initiative; Apple visionOS HIG

rule.rationale: Subtitles are an accessibility floor; absence excludes deaf and HoH users.

rule.statement: Turning MUST default to snap-turn for novice settings. Smooth turn MUST be user-selectable with documented angular-velocity cap.
rule.rationale: Smooth turn triggers more nausea than smooth locomotion; snap-turn is the safer default.
